steps.title: Find angle C in triangle ABC using angle sum
steps.content: Angle $A = 27°$ and angle $B = 90°$ are given. Angles in a triangle sum to $180°$, so: $$C = 180° - 27° - 90° = 63°$$

steps.title: Use congruence to find angle F
steps.content:
The congruence specifies that $A$ corresponds to $D$. Since angle $B = 90°$ and angle $E = 90°$, vertex $B$ corresponds to vertex $E$. Therefore, vertex $C$ corresponds to vertex $F$.
In congruent triangles, corresponding angles are equal, so angle $F = 63°$.
